title: Dumb question why was Shinji alone at the start of Eva
display_title: Dumb question why was Shinji alone at the start of Eva
wikitext:
[]: Dumb question: why was Shinji alone at the start of Eva?             Quote Post by popcorn » Sun Nov 15, 2015 7:01 am Hi everyone. I've registered here just to find an answer to a dumb question that's haunted me for years. Shinji arrives in Tokyo, a heavily fortified city, and waits to be picked up by Misato. In that time, a state of emergency is declared, and everyone hauls ass to a shelter. So why the hell is Shinji standing around outside like an idiot, waiting to get stamped on? Is this a plot hole or just something that I don't get?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Nov 15, 2015 11:03 am I'd assumed that he was the only one getting off the train at that stop; all the automated services were still running, but nobody was around.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Sun Nov 15, 2015 11:38 am And he's a hick country boy, new to Tokyo-3, and quite likely unaware of the Angel precautions and shelters.

[]:             Quote Post by unz » Sun Nov 15, 2015 11:57 am I thought Misato was late and he didn't know where the shelter was but I don't remember the details. I remember boobs on a postcard. Maybe he was busy

[]:             Quote Post by xPearse » Sun Nov 15, 2015 8:02 pm Yes, it is a plot hole. A very big one. Just like how did Shinji work with Unit-02 when they were on Over the Rainbow or whatever the fuck you called it. Nah. I wouldn't think too hard of it inverse, it's just a plot device like everything. Even more so seeing how the plot and characters are second to the meaning of the show. unz wrote: I thought Misato was late and he didn't know where the shelter was but I don't remember the details. I remember boobs on a postcard. Maybe he was busy

[]:             Quote Post by popcorn » Mon Nov 16, 2015 1:19 pm Thanks for the replies, everyone. None of the explanations really satisfy me to be honest, so I guess it's a plot hole after all. xPearse wrote: I wouldn't think too hard of it inverse, it's just a plot device like everything. Even more so seeing how the plot and characters are second to the meaning of the show.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Nov 16, 2015 1:33 pm Mr. Tines wrote: I'd assumed that he was the only one getting off the train at that stop; all the automated services were still running, but nobody was around.

[]:             Quote Post by MarioAyanami128 » Mon Nov 16, 2015 1:34 pm I have to disagree. Misato says she is preety late to pick him up. I think it's preety safe to assume that they arranged the meeting in Tokyo 3. The Manga, while not a direct representation of what happens in NGE, kinda gives an idea of what the director wanted for the audience to infer. While not explained to the viewer, I don't think that it's a plot hole. Evangelion has a distinct detail, that its beginning is minimalist, and goes right to the point, with few introductions, in a matter that the audience can find itself in the plot and what is going on based on their own judgement. ^^

[]:             Quote Post by popcorn » Mon Nov 16, 2015 2:03 pm NemZ wrote: This. How would he have any idea what was going on or where he was supposed to go?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Nov 16, 2015 2:14 pm Misato is ALSO late, yes, but Shinji is still just wandering around in a completely empty train station. We don't see any crowds of people fleeing, just him, so presumably they've already all gotten to shelter before he arrived in the middle of things with no clue what is going on. Shutting down the trains in an emergency isn't going to happen when the key to resolving the emergency needs to get where he's going on a train... surely Nerv could pull some strings to make sure that single train keeps moving.

[]:             Quote Post by popcorn » Mon Nov 16, 2015 2:24 pm NemZ wrote: Misato is ALSO late, yes, but Shinji is still just wandering around in a completely empty train station. We don't see any crowds of people fleeing, just him, so presumably they've already all gotten to shelter before he arrived in the middle of things with no clue what is going on. Shutting down the trains in an emergency isn't going to happen when the key to resolving the emergency needs to get where he's going on a train... surely Nerv could pull some strings to make sure that single train keeps moving.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Mon Nov 16, 2015 3:23 pm Gendou held off summoning Shinji as long as he could (part of the trying to spare his son side of him that people occasionally mention and acknowledge, I suppose), but the Angel turned up that bit too soon. Just coincidence to suit the plot, and not requiring any more explanation than that.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Nov 16, 2015 3:29 pm popcorn wrote: Like, why didn't the Nerv secret service guys just show up at his house and say "Hey kid, get in the helicopter?"

[]:             Quote Post by Rei IV » Mon Nov 16, 2015 3:44 pm This is the first time I ever heard Shin-chan is from the country side or is a hick. Sauce?

[]:             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Mon Nov 16, 2015 4:06 pm Where the heck IS Shinji when he gets off the train anyway? Seems like they have a fairly long drive before getting back into Tokyo-3.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Mon Nov 16, 2015 4:14 pm Rei IV wrote: This is the first time I ever heard Shin-chan is from the country side or is a hick. Sauce?

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Nov 16, 2015 6:34 pm xPearse wrote: Yes, it is a plot hole. A very big one.

[]:             Quote Post by Iuvenal » Mon Nov 16, 2015 6:56 pm I'm late to this party, but I'd say the reason is that "NERV is not a well-run organization". It's one of about a dozen times that a "rocks fall, everyone dies" ending was averted by serendipity.

[]:             Quote Post by unz » Tue Nov 17, 2015 2:07 am I second the minimal thing.

[]:             Quote Post by Compiling_Autumn » Tue Nov 17, 2015 3:18 am popcorn wrote: He would follow the masses of people rushing to shelters and evacuation zones. e: In the event of emergency, Tokyo trains stop immediately.
